atomicity.message: Staged payload is exactly the land-05 administrative tracker import: only .beads/issues.jsonl is staged, produced from the current Beads state, preserving historical and current tracker records as history rather than implementation evidence. Source, docs, release, binary, and unrelated working-tree changes remain unstaged.

conditions.message: The staged payload is tracker-state JSONL only. I found no lint/style implementation, lint command configuration, formatter/static-analysis rule, or code surface governed by lint-rules in this staged diff.
evidence: git diff --cached --name-status -> only M .beads/issues.jsonl is staged.
git diff --cached --stat -- .beads/issues.jsonl -> 1 file changed, 13 insertions(+).
go run ./cmd/burpvalve lint -> status not_enforced, command_count 0, message 'no executable lint commands declared; lint-rules wishlist skipped'.
sed -n '1,220p' backpressure/lint-rules.md -> lint-rules is a policy wishlist unless exact executable lint_commands/analyzers are declared.

conditions.message: The governed tracker-state surface was inspected. The 13 added JSONL records are self-contained beads/decisions; repeated launch/gate wording is intentional issue context, not duplicated implementation logic or divergent parallel behavior.
evidence: comm -13 <(git show HEAD:.beads/issues.jsonl | jq -r .id | sort) <(git show :.beads/issues.jsonl | jq -r .id | sort) -> 13 added IDs: land-04, land-05, OSL decisions/tasks, OXP tasks, VORCH tasks.

added-record summary command -> added rows are issue records with labels such as land/open-source-launch/oxp/vorch and statuses closed/in_progress/open; no source code or helper implementation is staged.

sed -n '1,220p' backpressure/dry.md -> condition targets copied code blocks, duplicated logic/validation/prompts, and premature abstractions; inspected staged rows do not introduce implementation duplication.

conditions.message: The staged tracker import is consistent with its own claims: it explicitly says it is tracker-state history, not proof that historical work landed in this git history. I did not find a shortcut claim that old historical beads are delivery evidence for later commits.
evidence: git show :.beads/issues.jsonl | jq -r 'select(.id=="project-readiness-kit-land-05-import-beads-state-e2s") | .description' | rg -n 'not as proof|forbids rewriting|Stage the Beads JSONL exactly|Historical closed beads|Fresh `land:` beads|No later landing commit|administrative tracker import' -> land-05 states tracker state is not proof of landed code, forbids rewriting/renaming, preserves historical records, and says later commits may cite only fresh land beads.
comm-added-ID check -> added records include historical/current planning state and current land records; land-05 is in_progress, not falsely closed.
git show :.beads/issues.jsonl | jq -c land-04 close record + git rev-parse HEAD -> land-04 close reason's commit equals current HEAD 2411d3dba3e9d33dbd50788a5e0e6f566795e7bd.
sed -n '1,260p' backpressure/anti-reward-hacking.md -> condition purpose is preventing superficial success signals, fake completion patterns, metric gaming, and requiring evidence.

conditions.message: The staged tracker import defines done surfaces explicitly for the active land-05 bead and imported plan beads; land-05 is still in_progress and does not claim completion before the commit lands.
evidence: git show :.beads/issues.jsonl | jq -c 'select(.id=="project-readiness-kit-land-05-import-beads-state-e2s") | {id,title,status,description,acceptance_criteria,labels}' -> land-05 status is in_progress and description lists acceptance criteria: only .beads/issues.jsonl staged, produced by br sync --flush-only, administrative tracker import, later delivery beads are current land beads.

added-record summary command -> project-readiness-kit-land-05-import-beads-state-e2s has status in_progress; project-readiness-kit-land-04-planning-round-docs-f7b is the only added closed land record.
sed -n '1,220p' backpressure/definition-of-done.md -> condition purpose covers checks, review, logs, commits, beads, PRs, deployment evidence, and risk notes.

conditions.message: The staged import is honest about tracker evidence: it imports tracker history rather than turning historical beads into proof. The new land-04 close evidence names the actual current HEAD commit and an attestation that exists in HEAD.
evidence: git show :.beads/issues.jsonl | jq -c 'select(.id=="project-readiness-kit-land-04-planning-round-docs-f7b") | {id,title,status,closed_at,close_reason,labels}' -> land-04 is closed with close_reason naming commit 2411d3dba3e9d33dbd50788a5e0e6f566795e7bd and attestation backpressure/attestations/2fe639451c17f25b7744d2469ba8ed80327be12a88242fc8fb517137555d4614.json.
git rev-parse HEAD -> 2411d3dba3e9d33dbd50788a5e0e6f566795e7bd.
git cat-file -e HEAD:backpressure/attestations/2fe639451c17f25b7744d2469ba8ed80327be12a88242fc8fb517137555d4614.json && echo attestation-present-in-HEAD -> attestation-present-in-HEAD.

git show :.beads/issues.jsonl | jq -r 'select(.id=="project-readiness-kit-land-05-import-beads-state-e2s") | .description' | rg -n 'not as proof|administrative tracker import|not manual JSON editing' -> land-05 says tracker state is not proof of landed code and must be treated as administrative tracker import.

conditions.message: The governed security/privacy surface was inspected. The staged tracker import does not expose secret material; secret-related matches are audit threshold prose or future launch gating language, and public-launch beads keep visibility/security work gated behind owner decisions and anonymous verification.
evidence: git show :.beads/issues.jsonl | rg -n 'secret|token|credential|password|PRIVATE|BEGIN PRIVATE|id_rsa|api[_-]?key' -> matches were policy/plan text such as 'secrets/tokens/creds always block' and launch private/public wording; no private key, password, credential value, or token blob observed.
added-row secret scan with jq+rg -> matches appear in OSL owner-decision/audit-threshold records and launch constraints, not as credential material.
git show :.beads/issues.jsonl | jq -c added-record summaries -> OSL security-sensitive work is open decision/task state, not executed visibility flip or external publication.
sed -n '1,220p' backpressure/security-boundaries.md -> condition governs auth, secrets, tenant isolation, PII, permissions, sandbox, and external API safety.
